title: Unify Rootbroker recovery freshness decisions
goal: Make recovery status and privileged Rootbroker execution consume one canonical, atomically refreshed freshness record so a ready recovery status cannot coexist with a stale-marker execution denial for the same observation window.

acceptance.assertion: grabowski_recovery_status and privileged execution read the same canonical recovery record, max-age contract and digest-bound source identity; outputs expose bounded generated-at, age, max-age and typed freshness reason without secrets.

When the canonical record is ready and fresh, an eligible read-only privileged call is not denied as recovery-marker stale; stale, missing, malformed and future-dated records block both surfaces with coherent typed reasons.
Recovery refresh writes are atomic and readers cannot consume a partial or previous-generation marker after a successful ready result; race-focused tests cover refresh/check interleavings.
The fix does not weaken command allowlists, audit, kill-switch, recovery requirements or privilege boundaries; no stale marker may be ignored merely to make a call succeed.
After reviewed merge and deployment, one bounded read-only Rootbroker call succeeds while recovery_status reports the same fresh record ready, with matching freshness identity and an audit receipt.
Focused tests cover ready, stale, missing, malformed, future-clock, partial-refresh and concurrent-reader cases plus denial without command execution.

The installed Rootbroker keeps ProtectHome enabled while an exact read-only systemd exception exposes only the configured recovery source record and required parent traversal; the service can validate that source without broader home-directory visibility.

Under an identity-bound non-writable root-owned parent, the publisher repairs an obsolete non-root lock owner only after acquiring the descriptor-bound lock and atomically replaces an unsafe regular single-link legacy marker, while preserving generation rollback, collision, symlink, hardlink, kill-switch and digest checks.

metadata.source: OPERATOR-PC-HYGIENE-V1-T004 listener/firewall attribution on 2026-07-11; fresh Grabowski recovery publication reproduction on 2026-07-14
observed_contradiction: The fixed SSH-tunnelled restic probe completed backup, restore and repository check for heimberry:rest-server/grabowski-recovery-probe and wrote a fresh valid user-owned source record.
The same operation failed when publishing that source through the root-owned broker; the request service exited status 2/INVALIDARGUMENT and the client returned exit code 1.

The installed broker unit has ProtectHome=yes and no installed drop-in exposing the configured source path under /home/alex, while recovery_status remains fail-closed with canonical freshness reason unsafe-file.
does_not_establish: future privileged actions remain authorized only by their current recovery, audit, kill-switch and allowlist checks
manual or environment kill switches can be self-cleared; they intentionally remain an external operator boundary
cryptographic attestation against the trusted local owner beyond the documented root-owned files, digests and tamper-evident audit chain
that client tool snapshots automatically refresh after every server deployment

local_validation: 1542 tests passed with one expected skip
root_cutover_helper_validation: 1563 repository tests passed with one expected skip
commit-bound live read-only plan against installed root configuration returned ready=true

root_cutover_acceptance: Back up and SHA-256-bind the installed root broker, wrapper, systemd unit/drop-ins and privileged-actions configuration before mutation.
Install the reviewed broker contract and exact read-only recovery-source visibility drop-in while retaining ProtectHome and all unrelated sandbox restrictions.
Publish a fresh canonical record as root:root mode 0644 and verify schema, target, generation, source digest and canonical digest by readback.
Verify grabowski_recovery_status and the Rootbroker power gate report the same generated-at, age, max-age, source digest, canonical digest and ready reason.
Run one bounded read-only privileged /usr/bin/true proof and preserve the audit receipt; separately prove kill-switch denial.
Preserve a rollback receipt and do not weaken allowlists, audit, kill-switch, recovery requirements or privilege boundaries.

merged_disjoint_self_heal_slice.full_validation: make validate passed: 1588 tests, policy, context, runtime lock, deploy tooling and secret checks

metadata.former_block_reason: Fresh restore evidence exists, but the installed root-owned Rootbroker service still runs with ProtectHome=yes and no exact recovery-source visibility drop-in for the configured source record under /home/alex. The official publisher therefore exits fail-closed before canonical readback. Applying the reviewed service/drop-in cutover remains a root-bound operation. A separate disjoint self-heal slice covers obsolete safe marker and lock ownership, but does not replace this sandbox cutover.
metadata.former_next_action: Finish and independently review the exact Rootbroker recovery-source systemd drop-in/cutover, install it through the authorized root boundary, then run a fresh server recovery probe. Verify canonical mode and ownership, same-record status and broker readiness, one bounded /usr/bin/true proof and kill-switch denial. In parallel, finish the disjoint recovery-marker self-heal slice and merge it only after focused and full validation.

former_blocked_live_reproduction.canonical_error: canonical recovery record cannot be opened safely

former_live_root_cutover_gap.canonical_status_reason: unsafe-file: canonical recovery record cannot be opened safely
former_live_root_cutover_gap.installed_publisher_action_presence: not independently readable from the root-owned configuration
former_live_root_cutover_gap.source_visibility_causal_assessment: high confidence from exact unit configuration and source location; exact broker exception unavailable

former_root_cutover_helper_state.socket_preflight: requires an already active Rootbroker socket and treats unknown systemctl state as an error before backup or mutation
former_root_cutover_helper_state.platform_apply_result: blocked before process start by platform security checks
former_root_cutover_helper_state.remaining_boundary: explicit desktop or otherwise authorized root execution of the reviewed helper

metadata.resolution_summary: The reviewed Rootbroker cutover is installed with an exact recovery-source systemd drop-in; the canonical root marker is fresh, root-owned and source-digest aligned; bounded privileged execution succeeds; kill-switch state now blocks both status readiness flags; and an audit-bound Grabowski test switch can be safely quarantined without weakening manual or environment emergency stops.
metadata.verification_note: T030 is satisfied: the root sandbox exposes only the exact source and optional kill-switch paths; the canonical marker and current source are digest-aligned; bounded root execution succeeds; kill-switch engagement blocks both readiness surfaces; and only an audit-bound test marker can be autonomously quarantined with rollback-on-audit-failure.
